issue_title: Handlers are loaded differently from Collectors
issue_body:
All collectors are disabled by default. The "collectors_config_path" option under the 'server' section allows a user to specify a directory to read collector config files from, without having any references to collector names in the 'main' config file (as long as the collector-specific config file name matches an available collector class name). Inversely, all handlers are enabled by default. The "handlers_config_path" option under the 'server' section does the same thing as 'collectors_config_path', however the handler has to already be referenced in the 'handlers' field under the 'server' section. Only handlers listed in 'handlers' will be loaded. Is there a reason for this difference? From my current understanding of Diamond, it's not clear to me why these should differ.

Another related issue I've been experiencing is that it's not possible to disable handlers by setting enabled to False. If you want a handler to be disabled, it has to be removed from the 'handlers' option entirely. The main reason for this seems to be handler implementations don't seem to override the base Handler's 'self.enabled = True' (at least, the handlers I've looked at). So despite having 'enabled=False' defined in either the 'main' config file under handlers/default or handlers/<handler_name> or in a handler-specific config file, it never seems to take effect. And again, this default behavior differs from collectors which are disabled by default.

Any help on this would be much appreciated, thanks.

issue_title: JSON response decoding error with InfluxDB 0.8
issue_body:
I tried to use the InfluxDBClient to query InfluxDB v0.8.8 and get the following error:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./test.py", line 4, in <module>
    result = client.query('select * from /.*/ limit 1', chunked=True)
  File "/home/matt/projects/influxdb-python-test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/influxdb/influxdb08/client.py", line 419, in query
    chunked=chunked)
  File "/home/matt/projects/influxdb-python-test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/influxdb/influxdb08/client.py", line 451, in _query
    return list(chunked_json.loads(response.content.decode()))
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0x8b in position 1: ordinal not in range(128)
```

The response returned by the InfluxDB is chunked and also compressed with gzip. It seems that the client tries to parse the gzipped data as JSON. 

I am using the client for InfluxDB 0.8 as you can see below.
```python
from influxdb.influxdb08 import InfluxDBClient                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                              
client = InfluxDBClient('localhost', 8086, 'myusername', 'mypassword', 'mydatabase')                                                                                                                                
result = client.query('select * from /.*/ limit 1')                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                              
print("Result: {0}".format(result)) 
```

Relevant HTTP headers as sent in the request:
```
Accept: text/plain
Content-Type: application/json
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
```
Relevant HTTP headers as received in the response:
```
Content-Encoding: gzip
Content-Type: application/json
X-Influxdb-Version: InfluxDB v0.8.8 (git: afde71e) (leveldb: 1.15)
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
```
Python package versions:
```
$ pip list
influxdb (2.0.2)
requests (2.6.1)
```

Shouldn't the Python InfluxDB (or the requests library) decompress the response before parsing? Or should the InfluxDB API send a response with uncompressed data?

issue_title: Caches on docket report do not get cleared
issue_body:
This is a fun corner case we haven't run into! If we do something like this:

    report = DocketReport(court='foo')
    for query_param_dict in query_parameter_dicts:
        report.query()
        report.data

We'll have a big issue. The problem is that the first time we use `report.data`, it will parse out the content and then cache it, which is fine. But the next time we use it, it will query PACER (incurring a fee), make a new tree of the HTML from PACER, and then just use all the old data.

A better way to handle this is to clear the caches any time a parse operation is run.

issue_title: Implementing Constraints for the Unit Commitment
issue_body:
Consider the Unit Commitment problem with decision variables for each hour as 0/1 using MIP = 1. Meaning that for gen_commited[t] = 1 the generating unit will be on for the given point in time of the optimization.

A general constraint for the unit commitment problem is the minimum up/down time which determines that a generating unit must be on of off for a minimum time. I am working on just getting two hours tied to each other using PuLP as the following:

_The general formulation:_
gen_committed [t+1] >= gen_committed [t] - gen_committed [t-1] meaning that if the hour [t] is committed, the hour [t+1] must also be committed, this extends to further hours.

_PuLP implementation:_
for t in time:
       gen_committed[min(t+1,h-1)] >= gen_committed[t] - gen_committed[t-1]

The min() is used in order to limit the list to the given timespan (h-1), where time = h

The constraint ain't doing its job and simply wont work as i want it to. Further, the "PULP_CBC_CMD"  does return strange statuses for this implementation, sometimes infeasible, sometimes optimal. 


Can anyone help me with where i go wrong?

issue_title: Symlink Support
issue_body:
Here is my proposal for how we should add Transfer's upcoming support of symlinks to the SDK:

**Task submission:**

Add `recursive_symlink="ignore"` to TransferData.__init__`'s named arguments, then add it as one of the `TransferData`'s query paramaters.

SDK will not enforce that the value is in "ignore", "keep", or "copy" to stay in our model of giving back the errors Transfer gives us.

Add `add_symlink_item(self, source_path, destination_path)` to `transferData`'s methods, akin to `TransferDat.add_item` that adds a `transfer_symlink_item` to the DATA field of the submission data.

(could also add `symlink=False` to the existing `add_item` method, but a seperate method avoids the issue of `recursive` and `symlink` both being True.)

No code changes to `DeleteData` `TransferClient.submit_transfer` or `TransferClientsubmit_delete` should be needed.

**Task Monitoring:**

No code changes should be needed for task monitoring as we just return a `TransferResponse` that will now happen to have a few more data fields.

**Symink Creation:**

add an `operation_symlink(self, endpoint_id, symlink_target, path, **params)` method to `TransferClient`.

That makes a POST request to /operation/endpoint/<endpoint_id>/symlink with the given request parameters and returns a `TransferResponse`.

issue_title: Reconsider certain uses of property testing
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

Though there is clear value in using property testing for certain kinds of tests (such as end-to-end encoding tests), I've recently had some doubts about some other ways we're using property testing.  For example:
https://github.com/ethereum/eth-abi/blob/master/tests/encoding/test_encoder_properties.py#L327-L395

To a large extent, that property test and others like it seem to be testing second implementations of functionality against the original implementations.  This doesn't provide much value since the original implementation could have a flaw which wouldn't be revealed by testing it against a second implementation that uses the same logic.

### How can it be fixed?

It seems like we should replace these kinds of property tests with traditional tests that check for correct behavior given specific inputs.

issue_title: Tutorial: how to use filters?
issue_body:
* Version: 3.16.2
* Python: 3.5
* OS: linux

### What was wrong?
The example **filter** code just ended, without any output.

### How can it be fixed?

It took me ages to get your very first example working. I did not know what to do with the resulting `new_block_filter` object. 

I suspected I had to start some kind of eventloop, but I did not know how.  Finally I tried `.join()`, but the output was always nothing. Then suddenly for `.join(timeout=5)`, I got results, but only *AFTER* those 5 seconds, not already when the block appeared. 

Then, for this issue, when I replaced your `sys.stdout.write()` with `print()` ... it suddenly worked. What how why? It made me realize how to fix [your example](http://web3py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/filters.html#block-and-transaction-filters). Just add the \*grrr\* line:

```
import sys
from web3 import Web3, HTTPProvider, __version__
print (__version__) # or what is a better way?
web3 = Web3(HTTPProvider('http://localhost:8545'))

def blockfilter():
    
    def new_block_callback(block_hash):
        sys.stdout.write("New Block: {0}\n".format(block_hash))
        sys.stdout.flush() # without this NOTHING happened *grrr*
            
    new_block_filter = web3.eth.filter('latest')
    new_block_filter.watch(new_block_callback)
    
    return new_block_filter 

bf = blockfilter()

bf.join()  # mention at least this in your docs?
```

I suggest you give this example code somewhere on [filters.html](http://web3py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/filters.html), to show *how to use* such filter greenlets.

Thanks a lot. Very useful library your Web3.py. Keep up the good work.

issue_title: Proxy errors that aren't caused by a proxy
issue_body:
From a customer:
> conda wrongly reports that the proxy is not set when it can't reach a channel.

> Reason of the problem is actually that the proxy expects requests to be routed to the internet. There is no route from the proxy to the lab's LAN. Therefore conda cannot reach the anaconda server within the lab’s LAN.

> The workaround has been to add a NO_PROXY environment variable and exclude the lab's network domain. IP ranges does not seems to be accepted.

> However it would have been easier to understand what the problem is if errors were reported more accurately

issue_title: Support channel prefix in environment.yaml
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [ ] bug report - [x] feature request It'd be nice if `conda env create` supported the `channel::package` syntax in the `dependencies` list. ## Current Behavior Unclear, but I think that any channels detected as a prefix are *pre-pended* to the list of channels, and the install proceeds. ### Steps to Reproduce Given this `environment.yaml` file, ``` name: test-env dependencies: - python=3.6 - flask - conda-forge::requests ``` ## Expected Behavior I'd expect python and flask to come from the highest priority channel (defaults), and requests to come from conda-forge. It looks like conda-forge was added as the highest priority. I'd further expect that the prefix applies *only* to requests (and its dependencies?). issue_title: Can't update conda
Anaconda.org over capacity
issue_body:
We had an automated test on Travis fail today while installing a conda package, because the anaconda.org server was at capacity:

`Fetching package metadata .......Error: HTTPError: 503 Server Error: Service Unavailable: Back-end server is at capacity for url: https://conda.anaconda.org/bioconda/linux-64/repodata.json.bz2: https://conda.anaconda.org/bioconda/linux-64/`

This has happened a few times now. Is anaconda.org is rate-limited by account/channel, or are the limits global? Is there any sort of CDN in front of the servers hosting packages? Is there a time of day that's best for running automated tests that depend on conda packages?

issue_title: git clone with --depth=1 in conda-build
issue_body: if you're using conda-build with a recipe that uses github as the source, the in most cases, there isn't much point in downloading the whole repository history. The whole history of mature repositories can be orders of magnitude larger than the size of a working copy. It would be good to at least have the option to clone with `--depth=1` (shallow clone) when building. Personally, I think that it should be the default.

issue_title: conda --remove corrupts ~/.condarc
issue_body:
`conda --remove` seems to write out YAML files w/o leading whitespaces in lists, a format that conda's config file reader can't read (as mentioned in #632).

Here's how to reproduce:
```
[mjuric@gallifrey ~]$ cat ~/.condarc
channels:
  - http://eupsforge.net/conda/nextgen
  - defaults

[mjuric@gallifrey ~]$ conda config --remove channels http://eupsforge.net/conda/nextgen --force

[mjuric@gallifrey ~]$ cat ~/.condarc
channels:
- defaults

[mjuric@gallifrey ~]$ conda config --add channels http://eupsforge.net/conda/nextgen
Error: Could not parse the yaml file. Use -f to use the
yaml parser (this will remove any structure or comments from the existing
.condarc file). Reason: couldn't parse modified yaml
```

issue_title: conda env create --dry
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...**
  - [ ] bug report
  - [X] feature request


## Current Behavior
`conda create --dry` exists

# Expected Behavior
It would be great to have also a `dry` mode for `conda env create`
It miss to us because we make ansible install some conda env after adding a conda environment .yaml in a git repository. With a dry mode, we should be able to check the syntax and the package availabilities before having to run the creating on our server.

I hope I'm clear enough.

Otherwise, great job. Thanks to contributors.

issue_title: Event handling in case of multiple timelines
Monitoring motors wires
issue_body:
As motors wires are under estimated (max current =1A) ; **lot** of connectivity issues come from old wires which become resistant.
It is possible to mesure the voltage at each motor (present_voltage register),  and with some load a difference of potential can be seen between two motors (some time we can see 1V between two motors) which is the sign of a high resistance cable (~Ω).
We can could add a primitive to to monitors this with a low frequency and send warning log.

issue_title: Can't use unicode string as where clause
issue_body:
On Python 2.7 passing a unicode string as the query to `where` fails with an error like:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<pyshell#5>", line 1, in <module>
    bcBasic.where("STATE=='California'", outcols=["nrow__", "GISJOIN"])
  File "c:\users\brenbarn\documents\python\extensions\bcolz\bcolz\ctable.py", line 852, in where
    "only boolean expressions or arrays are supported")
ValueError: only boolean expressions or arrays are supported
```

But using a bytestring works.

This is probably because of [this code](https://github.com/Blosc/bcolz/blob/0a061e2cf3f77c3a1493f26aada8bb56c6785b5f/bcolz/ctable.py#L845) in ctable.py, which explicitly checks if the query type `is str`.

I use Python 2.7 with `from __future__ import unicode_literals` and it is annoying to have to specify bytestrings.  I don't know if there are deeper reasons why unicode won't work (e.g., when passing to numexpr), but it would be nice.  If there are such deeper reasons, one possibility is to try encoding to ASCII (or whatever numexpr needs) and then raise a more informative error if it fails (e.g., "unicode string can't be used as query").  The current error message is confusing.

issue_title: Provide Installation Wheels
issue_body:
Hello,

installation of PySam takes really much time and it is a problem especially for continuous integration servers (e.g., Travis CI which is now very popular in combination with GitHub). It should be technically simple to create wheel files of PySam to make its installation faster (http://pythonwheels.com/).

See this thread: https://github.com/ga4gh/server/issues/71
They are building this PySam package: http://www.well.ox.ac.uk/~jk/wheelhouse.tar.

It would be very nice if there existed an official wheel file for PySam which people could use in their projects. For example, I cannot use the Oxford one since they create it only for Python 2.7.

Best

Karel Brinda

issue_body:
I'm pretty sure the culprit is the splitting section here https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/blob/master/pysam/libchtslib.pyx#L656-L657

For example:

```
>>> region = 'HCV-1:9400-9500'
>>> re.split('[:-]', region)
['HCV', '1', '9400', '9500']
>>> parts = re.split('[:-]', region)
>>> contig = parts[0]

'HCV'

And in pysam fetch:

issue_title: [Test] Tests written with older test base will no longer record
issue_body:
After my changes to the resource module, I decided to wipe the recordings and simply re-record all tests. However, upon doing so, I found that, after recent changes, tests written using the old test base (VCRTestBase) will no longer record when played back using:

`nosetests azure.cli.command_modules.resource.tests.test_resource.ResourceGroupNoWaitScenarioTest -c nose.cfg`

As a result, the test passed (it ran live but didn't record) and then failed on CI.  This leaves me with two options:

1. Convert all of the tests to the new test base (did for a few)
2. Restore the old recording (finally resorted to this)

Considering the plan is for the live tests suite to update the recordings, this is a major bug since only tests that have the new base will work.

issue_title: Improve utility of Help parsing errors
issue_body:
I spent over an hour trying to diagnose failing tests due to a help file entry labeled "group" instead of "command". Trying to add a simple example via the help file also caused hours of debugging and delay for what ended up being indentation issues. 

Given that we expect non-programmers to contribute to the _help.py file via the doc edit link, we really need to improve the parsing process to give clear, actionable errors.

issue_title: az vm capture prints to stdout rather than returning values...
issue_body:
...which breaks output formatters, multiple ids etc. I'm also not sure why we have our own poller etc. (but I'm guessing that is because we are not returning values and letting the rest of the infrastructure do the right thing)

Current implementation:

def capture_vm(resource_group_name, vm_name, vhd_name_prefix,
               storage_container='vhds', overwrite=True):
    '''Captures the VM by copying virtual hard disks of the VM and outputs a
    template that can be used to create similar VMs.
    :param str vhd_name_prefix: the VHD name prefix specify for the VM disks
    :param str storage_container: the storage account container name to save the disks
    :param str overwrite: overwrite the existing disk file
    '''
    client = _compute_client_factory()
    parameter = VirtualMachineCaptureParameters(vhd_name_prefix, storage_container, overwrite)
    poller = client.virtual_machines.capture(resource_group_name, vm_name, parameter)
    result = LongRunningOperation()(poller)
    **print(json.dumps(result.output, indent=2)) # pylint: disable=no-member**

issue_title: Cannot set reverse fqdn for public ip without dnsSettings
issue_body:
If I create public ip (az network public-ip create ...) without RR dns like abcdef I am not able to set PTR record because the whole dnsSettings section is missing. I need to create/update (az network public-ip update --dns-name abcdef -n NAMEOFIP -g RGROUP) the IP name first and after this step I am able to set PTR.

Err:
The domain name label in the dnsSettings section of the public IP address NAMEOFIP is null or empty. If a dnsSettings section exists, its domain name label must not be null or empty.

Env:
mc os x high sierra with the latest updates
azure-cli (2.0.31)

The solution could be
a) always create RR in the creating phase (do not allow to create IP without it)
b) add random name to the creation phase in case the user used none
c) allow to have empty RR/missing dnsSettings section and create it in the PTR process creation

issue_title: Prototype Positional Arguments
issue_body:
Prototype positional arguments for `group delete`.

**Required**
- [x] Core modifications
- [ ] Telemetry
- [x] Changes to help system - Positional args appear in alphabetical order rather than positional order.
- [x] Evaluate error message
- [x] Tab completion support
- [ ] Interactive support - command works, but TAB completion does not. It should `--resource-group` as a completion, which gives an "unrecognized argument" message if you try and use it.
- [ ] Reference docs
- [ ] Tests

issue_title: [DNS] 'record-set show' and 'record-set list' don't show sufficient data
issue_body:
Consider:

```
C:\Users\jonatul>az network dns record-set show -g rg1 -z jt-test.com -t A -n cli2 -o table
Name    ResourceGroup      Ttl
------  ---------------  -----
cli2    rg1               3600
```

And

```
C:\Users\jonatul>az network dns record-set list -g rg1 -z jt-test.com  -o table
Name                ResourceGroup      Ttl  Type    Metadata
------------------  ---------------  -----  ------  --------------------------------------
@                   rg1               3600  NS
@                   rg1               3600  SOA     s="fdf"
@                   rg1               3600  MX
*                   rg1                100  A
*                   rg1                100  TXT
*                   rg1                100  AAAA
*                   rg1                100  SRV
a                   rg1                300  A       dept="shopping" env="test"
cccname             rg1                100  CNAME
cli2                rg1               3600  A
```

Problems:

1. In both cases, the DNS records themselves are not shown! This is critical data, it must be shown. The record data can be formatted with 1 line per record (see example/details below).
2. For both `show` and `list`, showing 'ResourceGroup' is pointless and wastes space. They're within a zone, which is in the given RG. It will always have the same value, as specified on the command line. Please remove.
3. Metadata is not shown for the `show` command. It should be. Make it the same as for the `list` command
4. For consistency, I suggest also adding the 'Type' column to the output for the `show` command. I.e. the output of `show` and `list` will then be exactly the same. I know 'Type' is redundant for `show` since it's specified on the command line, but it's a narrow column and so I think it's worth it.  (It is standard practice for DNS records to be listed including their type)
5. Please order the columns as follows: Name, Type, TTL, Records, Metadata. That matches the standard sequence used by other DNS tools in the industry (e.g. 'dig')

Example output:

```
Name         Type   Ttl     Records                           Metadata
------------ ------ ------- --------------------------------- ------------------------------
@            SOA    86400   
@            NS     172800  ns1-01.azure-dns.com               dept="shop" env="test"
                            ns2-01.azure-dns.net
                            ns3-01.azure-dns.org
                            ns4-01.azure-dns.info
*            A      3600    1.2.3.4
                            5.6.7.8
foo          AAAA   60      2001:db8::4
bar          CNAME  0       www.microsoft.com
fish         MX     3600    10 mail1.microsoft.com
                            20 mail2.microsoft.com
worm         PTR    3600    reverse.contoso.com
dog          SRV    1800    10 100 80 foo.bar.com
                            20 200 80 bar.com
cat          TXT    360     "This string is truncated to fit."
                            "This is a second TXT record"
```

Notes:

1. Standard order of fields in SOA record is: email, host, serial, refresh, retry, expire, minTTL. I suggest simply laying them out in this sequence (space-separated) and truncating to fit.
2. In the above example, there are 4 NS records in the record set. This is normal. If they can't be laid out on separate lines, then lay them out on one line (semicolon separated) and truncate to fit.
3. Standard order of fields in MX record is: preference, exchange. Lay them out in this sequence (space separated). Again, I'd prefer one line per record, but if that isn't possible then lay out the records with semi-colon separators and truncated to fit.
4. Standard order of fields in SRV record is: priority, weight, port, target. As above, use spaces between fields, and if multiple records need to be on the same line use semicolons to separate them
5. Each TXT record set can contain multiple TXT records. And each TXT record can contain multiple strings.  **Within** a TXT record, those strings should be concatenated for display purposes. Please also put " " marks around the resulting string.  Separate TXT records should be displayed separately as above.
6. If you can right-align the Ttl, that would be awesome. Be sure to allow enough space for 6-digit Ttls.

issue_body:
Many pre-commit hooks are appropriate to run per file -- flake8 and autopep8 are great examples.

Others, for example mypy and cyclic import detection, need to be run on an entire repository to work. In these cases

* Changes to one file can affect other files
* splitting a check into independent subchecks with xargs is inappropriate

Any thoughts on how to work with these in pre-commit? Pre-commit features that are helpful when working with these hooks include:
* environment management
* common configuration / configuration centralization
* include/exclude configuration

Thanks!

issue_title: Multipart base64 encoding produces long line
issue_body:
## Long story short

<!-- Please describe your problem and why the fix is important. -->

When using the aiohttp.multipart module, and its MultipartWriter class, we can create multipart documents whose payload can be transfert-encoded in base64.

We use this module to retrieve some http content via aiohttp client, and _stream_ a multipart document containing the Responses provided by the client to another processus via stdout (so we're not buffering the contents into memory, but act like a proxy).

The MultipartWriter generates base64 subdocuments without newlines. In version 0.2.1 of aiohttp, it was with multiple lines.

I know the base64 format does not define line length, but :
- there are very few reasons to use the multipart module with base64 encoding in http transaction (since http can handle binary contents)
- there are very good reasons to use multipart + base 64 in emails since emails are 7 bits only
- emails format is described in RFC5322
- RFC5322 states that line length must be more than 998 characters (and should not be more than 78 characters) (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5322.html#section-2.1.1)
- RFC2045, which define MIME (englobing the multipart format), states that "The encoded output stream must be represented in lines of no more than 76 characters each. " (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2045.html#section-6.8)

I think the MultipartWriter should allow us to generate base64 sub contents with newlines (potentially with an option).

## Expected behaviour

<!-- What is the behaviour you expect? -->

When producing a multipart document with base64 tranfert encoding, the final document should not have lines longer than 78 characters (more or less).

## Actual behaviour

<!-- What's actually happening? -->

When producing a multipart document with base64 tranfert encoding, the final document has the entire base64 subdocument on one line.

## Steps to reproduce

<!-- Please describe steps to reproduce the issue.
     If you have a script that does that please include it here within
     markdown code markup -->

```
import sys

import asyncio
import aiohttp.multipart as mp

class Writer:
	@asyncio.coroutine
	def write(self, chunk):
		sys.stdout.buffer.write(chunk)


a = mp.MultipartWriter()
a.append("i" * 200, {"Content-transfer-encoding": "base64"})
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

loop.run_until_complete(a.write(Writer()))
```

## Fix

In aiohttp.multipart.MultipartPayloadWriter.write() method, the call to base64.b64encode could be replaced by base64.encodebytes which takes care of the newlines.

issue_title: Multidicts does not preserve order on update
issue_body:
When updating existing key in multidict, it is moved to the end (original order is not preserved):
```
>>> d = CIMultiDict()
>>> d['1'] = '2'
>>> d['3'] = '4'
>>> d
<CIMultiDict('1': '2', '3': '4')>
>>> d['1'] = '10'
>>> d
<CIMultiDict('3': '4', '1': '10')>
```
In above example, guarantee of preserving insertion order should leave existing key `'1'` on its original first position.

issue_title: Sleep and wake-up of inactive 'view's of a notebook.
issue_body:
This is a feature request.

After a browser session for the notebook is inactive for a long time, we shall put that window to sleep:

- the entire page is grayed out
- a pop up telling the user the session is sleeping and a button to wake it up.
- If the user clicks on 'wake up', the notebook is reloaded to the most recent content.

The main purpose is to minimize the risk overwriting changes the user done on the same notebook from another browser session. This happens quite often if the user works on more than one computers, or if the notebook is hosted on a cloud like environment via jupyterhub.

A similar feature was implemented in overleaf.com. I will try to upload a screenshot when I see one next time.

issue_title: Docs: Update the "Notebook Basics" example
issue_body:
I've found that the best explanation on using the notebooks UI for first time users is the **Notebook Basics** example, which contains crucial information (and screenshots!) of Dashboard, notebook, edit vs command mode, toolbar etc:

https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/docs/source/examples/Notebook/Notebook%20Basics.ipynb

This info is very important and not found anywhere else in the notebook documentation.

The first section (Running the Notebook Server, which discuss using non-default ports) is, however, distracting. User can use some publically hosted notebooks or may launch the notebook via the Anaconda launcher. I would separate how to launch the server from how to use the notebook interface.

So I propose to remove the first section and start with the section: The Notebook dashboard.

The paragraphs on launching the server can be moved to the main sphinx documentation, maybe expanding this section:

https://jupyter.readthedocs.org/en/latest/running.html#running-the-notebook

Also this important notebook should be highlighted better in the main sphinx docs.

issue_title: Getting `TypeError: float() argument must be a string or a number`
issue_body:
First of all, great library! I'm finding it very useful for some projects that I am working on.
However, in some instances I am running into a TypeError in models where an array is being sliced or assigned as a block within a larger array (despite being scalar functions ultimately). 

I can reproduce it with a stripped-down example:

https://gist.github.com/thearn/faba933208316d71cdb9

```python

import autograd.numpy as np
from autograd import grad

def f(A):

    B = np.zeros((4,4))

    B[:2, :2] = A

    return B.sum()

A = np.random.randn(2,2)

df = grad(f)

print df(A)

# expected: [[ 1.,  1.],[ 1.,  1.]]
```

which gives the traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/tristanhearn/Dropbox/code/adcomponent/src/adcomponent/test.py", line 17, in <module>
    print df(A)

    return backward_pass(*forward_pass(fun,args,kwargs,argnum))

issue_title: Recovering from a loss of data integrity / support for transactions
issue_body:
I'm running into some problems keeping data integrity between getstream and our database with transactions enabled, although the bigger problem is recovering from any loss of integrity. Problem is that at any point in the transaction the model could fail to save/delete and rollback, but the activity still remains in getstream. 

At the moment I've had to settle on a hacky solution which just filters out any activity that fails to be linked to an object during the enriching process, but this feels a little bit inadequate. Ideally there would be a management command that I could run during maintenance to remove any deleted models from getstream, something like haystack's rebuild/update index commands, but looking at the api I'm not sure if that's possible.

Do you have any general advice / is there a way to build in better support for transactions?

issue_title: [Bug Report] Wrong result got from get_distance method for torus continuous space
issue_body:
```
  def get_distance(self, pos_1, pos_2):
        """ Get the distance between two point, accounting for toroidal space.

        Args:
            pos_1, pos_2: Coordinate tuples for both points.

        """
        pos_1 = np.array(pos_1)
        pos_2 = np.array(pos_2)
        if self.torus:
            pos_1 = (pos_1 - self.center) % self.size
            pos_2 = (pos_2 - self.center) % self.size
        return np.linalg.norm(pos_1 - pos_2)
```
Above is the get_distance method for continuous space in mesa 0.8.1.

Although I'm not clear about the algorithm here, when my input is
```
pos_1 = (50, 40)
pos_2 = (50, 50)
size = (100, 100)
torus = True
```
I got the result 90 rather than 10. 
So I wonder if there is any problem about this algorithm.

issue_body:

##########################################
# My code at this point
from mesa import Agent, Model
from mesa.time import RandomActivation
from mesa.space import MultiGrid
from mesa.datacollection import DataCollector

import random
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

% matplotlib inline

class MoneyAgent(Agent):
    """An agent with fixed initial wealth."""
    def __init__(self, unique_id):
        self.unique_id = unique_id
        self.wealth = 1
        #print(self.unique_id)
        
    def step(self, model):
        # ...
        self.move(model)
        if self.wealth > 0:
            self.give_money(model)
    
    def move(self, model):
        possible_steps = model.get_neighborhood(self.pos, moore=True, include_center=False)
        new_position = random.choice(possible_steps)
        model.grid.move_agent(new_position)
    
    def give_money(self, model):
        cellmates = model.grid.get_cell_list_contents(self.pos)
        if len(cellmates) > 1:
            other = random.choice(cellmates)
            other.wealth += 1
            self.wealth -= 1
            
def compute_gini(model):
    agent_wealths = [agent.wealth for agent in model.schedule.agents]
    x = sorted(agent_wealths)
    N = model.num_agents
    B = sum(xi*(N-i) for i,xi in enumerate(x)) / N*sum(x)
    return 1 + (1/N) - 2*B

class MoneyModel(Model):
    """A model with a number of agents."""
    def __init__(self, N, width, height):
        self.num_agents = N
        self.running = True
        self.grid = MultiGrid(width, height, True)
        self.schedule = RandomActivation(self)
        self.datacollector = DataCollector(model_reporters={"Gini", compute_gini},
            agent_reporters={"Wealth": lambda a: a.wealth})
        # Create agents
        for i in range(self.num_agents):
            a = MoneyAgent(i)
            self.schedule.add(a)
            x = random.randrange(self.grid.width)
            y = random.randrange(self.grid.height)
            self.grid.place_agent(a, (x,y))
    
    def step(self):
        '''Advance model by one step.'''
        self.datacollector.collect(self)
        self.schedule.step()

# Running it with:
model = MoneyModel(50, 10, 10)

issue_title: Adding device to trust domain fails with BIG-IP version 13.0
issue_body:
When creating a cluster (e.g., active-standby HA pair), we see this error when using version 13.0: 

```
icontrol.exceptions.iControlUnexpectedHTTPError: 400 Unexpected Error: Bad Request for uri: https://10.190.24.98:443/mgmt/tm/sys/application/service/
Text: u'{"code":400,"message":"script did not successfully complete: (The specified IP address is an address on this device.\\n    while executing\\n\\"tmsh::modify cm trust-domain Root ca-devices add \\\\{ 192.168.1.245 \\\\} name bigip-1 username admin password admin\\" line:1)","errorStack":[],"apiError":3}'
```
It seems the tmsh modify cmd trust-domain command changed in 13.x.

According to https://support.f5.com/kb/en-us/products/big-ip_ltm/manuals/product/bigip-system-device-service-clustering-administration-13-0-0/13.html, the command in 13.x has this format:

`tmsh modify cm trust-domain Root add-device ca-devices true { ip_address } name device_name username admin password admin`

instead of this format currently used in the f5-sdk and which works in 11.x and 12.x:

`tmsh modify cm trust-domain Root ca-devices add { ip_address } name device_name username admin password admin `

issue_title: The webroot domain verification method fails with Hiawatha web server
issue_body:
The webroot domain verification method fails with Hiawatha web server because Hiawatha blocks all access to hidden directories (directories beginning with a dot) for security reasons. It will always return a 403 when /.well-known/acme-challenge/ is accessed.
See this discussion: https://www.hiawatha-webserver.org/forum/topic/623

issue_title: OCSP Stapling on Nginx breaks if not in the first server block
issue_body:
See https://trac.nginx.org/nginx/ticket/810.

What's the best way to make sure we put 'ssl_stapling on' in the first server block? Do we edit existing configs that are included into nginx.conf? Or do we add a dummy server block that's earliest in the nginx.conf file?

Note this is on a per-port basis, so e.g. if a config has listeners for :443 and :9443, we need to have ssl_stapling on in the first server block for each of those ports.

cc @diracdeltas

issue_title: Support SparkPost in django-anymail
issue_body:
This isn't an issue related directly to this repository, but it seemed like a good place to reach out to the developers at SparkPost on this issue.

With Mandrill now having no free tier, customers are looking to migrate to other ESPs. SparkPost seems like a good candidate, as it has a similar UI to Mandrill, has a generous free tier and a helpful migration tool. The latter is important, because businesses on the free tier probably don't want to spend a lot of time and money on switching.

It therefore makes sense that migrating the backend to SparkPost is easy too. [django-anymail](https://github.com/anymail/django-anymail) has made it easy to migrate from djrill, a package which supported only Mandrill, to its ESP-agnostic interface, making it easy to switch to any one of a number of supported ESPs. However, SparkPost is not currently supported.

This is a shame. With support for SparkPost in django-anymail, it would make it easier still for django users to migrate from Mandrill to SparkPost.

I've made [a request for its support on that repository](https://github.com/anymail/django-anymail/issues/11), but I also thought it would be worth raising it with you here.

issue_title: rerunning workflows with --cachedir sometimes repeats steps despite no changes to input
issue_body:
I have been looking at #71 for a description of how the `--cachedir` option works. As I understand various properties of the input files are encoded in a hash that is checked every time the workflow is rerun. If they match the previously calculated version is taken, otherwise it is recomputed (with all the steps that depend on that step).

I have tried to run a workflow a couple of times in succession without changing anything. However, some steps of the workflow are recomputed. I have also observed that the first step that is recomputed changes. I tried also to use the additional `--leave-tmpdir` parameter in combination and it seems to recompute less, however not consistently.

I am unsure whether I am using the feature incorrectly.

If this is unexpected behaviour, I am not too sure how to provide a reproducible example or what kind of diagnostics one would like to see to get to the bottom of this?

Thanks for the development!

issue_title: Setting choropleth layer name
issue_body:
Dearest all,

I've been using Folium for a group assignment and am loving what I can do with it so far. However, I run into troubles when trying to set the name for choropleth layers. While GeoJSON and TopoJSON formats have 'name' arguments that can be passed to the function, the choropleth function does not. As such, it appears as "macro_element_0a4d2f76a5a84a7084882de74056b4dd" in the layer list. I haven't found a passable argument or workaround for this problem.

I'm using the following code to add the choropleths:

```python
tweet_map.choropleth(geo_path = polygon_geojson, data = polygon_data,
             columns=['name', 'avgsentiment'],
             threshold_scale = [-0.25, -0.125, 0, 0.125, 0.25],
             key_on = 'feature.properties.name',
             fill_color = 'RdYlGn', fill_opacity = 0.7, line_opacity = 0.5,
             legend_name = 'average sentiment',
             reset=True)
```
Am I missing something obvious or is there no easy way to set the layer name?

Yours truly,
Alex

EDIT: Setting the legend name does not help. It still displays as 'macro_element'. Additionally, I should mention that I add two choropleths to the same map. Perhaps this could cause problems as well?

issue_title: GeoJSON popup not working when GeoJSON in FeatureGroup
issue_body:
Based on this example:
[](http://nbviewer.jupyter.org/github/python-visualization/folium/blob/master/examples/Highlight_Function.ipynb)

#### Please add a code sample or a nbviewer link, copy-pastable if possible

```python
import os
import folium
import pandas as pd

print(folium.__version__)

df = pd.read_csv('highlight_flight_trajectories.csv')

m = folium.Map(
    location=[40, 10],
    zoom_start=4,
    control_scale=True,
    prefer_canvas=True
)


def style_function(feature):
    return {
        'fillColor': '#ffaf00',
        'color': 'blue',
        'weight': 1.5,
        'dashArray': '5, 5'
    }


def highlight_function(feature):
    return {
        'fillColor': '#ffaf00',
        'color': 'green',
        'weight': 3,
        'dashArray': '5, 5'
    }

# Add Layer
layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name='layer')

for index, row in df.iterrows():
    c = folium.GeoJson(
        row['geojson'],
        name=('{}{}'.format(row['dep'], row['dest'])),
        overlay=True,
        style_function=style_function,
        highlight_function=highlight_function
    )
    folium.Popup('{}\n{}'.format(row['dep'], row['dest'])).add_to(c)
    c.add_to(layer)

layer.add_to(m)
folium.LayerControl().add_to(m)
m.save('maptest.html')

```
#### Problem description

Popup of a GeoJSON is not working, if the GeoJSON is contained in a FeatureGroup.

#### Expected Output

When clicking on a GeoJSON the popup should be displayed

issue_title: Need better configuration for tmux windows
issue_body: I would like to change the colors and format for the tmux line. Not the segments on the right, but the list of windows on the left. I can't find anything in the docs about how to configure the way the active/inactive windows are rendered. Does such configuration exist? Could it be added?

issue_title: download_file_by_name and download_file_by_id spool file in RAM
issue_body:
I and others have observed that when using download_file_by_name or download_file_by_id, the file you are downloading spools into RAM and only getting written to disk when it's fully downloaded. 

Therefore, a file larger than the amount of RAM in your system will crash. Is there a way to periodically flush the downloading file to disk?

issue_title: b2 upload-file --sha1 <hash> gives no error when receives wrong hash.
issue_body:
I'm trying to automate some single-file backup tasks, and I've found that while the web API apparently works, I don't know how much I can trust the b2 CLI. 

When I call the function with the correct SHA1 as argument:

>b2 upload-file --sha1 f871a33490af899cf5476c002c513d1bc4e537b9 futuroapiteste test_file.txt test_file_b2_cli.txt
test_file.txt: 100%|██████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 5.52k/5.52k [00:01<00:00, 5.42kB/s]
URL by file name: https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/futuroapiteste/test_file_b2_cli.txt
URL by fileId: https://f002.backblazeb2.com/b2api/v1/b2_download_file_by_id?fileId=4_zeee396220d1bff11694a0a1d_f107a92e113ff0c11_d20180731_m193133_c002_v0001095_t0024
{
  "action": "upload",
  "fileId": "4_zeee396220d1bff11694a0a1d_f107a92e113ff0c11_d20180731_m193133_c002_v0001095_t0024",
  "fileName": "arquivo_teste_b2_cli.txt",
  "size": 5520,
  "uploadTimestamp": 1533065493000
}



======================================================

When I call the function with the wrong SHA1 as argument:
>b2 upload-file --sha1 1111111111111111111111111111111111111111 futuroapiteste test_file.txt test_file_b2_cli_wrong_sha1.txt
test_file.txt: 100%|██████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 5.52k/5.52k [00:00<00:00, 6.10kB/s]
URL by file name: https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/futuroapiteste/test_file_b2_cli_wrong_sha1.txt
URL by fileId: https://f002.backblazeb2.com/b2api/v1/b2_download_file_by_id?fileId=4_zeee396220d1bff11694a0a1d_f116da3bb244871bc_d20180731_m193245_c002_v0001094_t0044
{
  "action": "upload",
  "fileId": "4_zeee396220d1bff11694a0a1d_f116da3bb244871bc_d20180731_m193245_c002_v0001094_t0044",
  "fileName": "arquivo_teste_b2_cli_wrong_sha1.txt",
  "size": 5520,
  "uploadTimestamp": 1533065565000
}

=======================================================
Using:
b2 command line tool, version 1.3.0

When I use b2 list-file-names, it shows me that b2 has the correct SHA1 for both files, which shows that the upload happened well, but then - 
Did it check the hash I submitted? 
If yes, why there was no error? 
(Maybe it recalculated the hash anyway before submitting...?)
(Does b2 upload-file already compare the locally created hash with the hash b2 calculates and returns when the file gets there...? )

The built-in CLI help states: 
>"By default, upload_file will compute the sha1 checksum of the file
    to be uploaded.  But, if you already have it, you can provide it
    on the command line to save a little time."

Which seems to suggest it does, but it doesn't explicitly say it _compares_, just 'computes', and the behavior would suggest otherwise.

issue_title: Method field should allow passing deserialization method only
issue_body:
Proposal:

- Rename `method_name` to `serialize` for better symmetry with `deserialize`.
- `serialize` should default to `None`.

Rationale:

Consistency with `Function` field. See https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/issues/325.

issue_title: proposal: add fields.Enum
issue_body:
Although marshmallow is not just for sqlalchemy, I think a reasonable standard for completeness is: "It can be used to serialize the basic types of sqlalchemy." One thing that's kinda inconvenient right now is enums. My current issue is with sqlalchemy columns constructed from pep 435 enums, although it applies equally to the enums themselves.

so I propose adding a new kind* of field, `Enum`, which would allow some syntax like

0. `fields.Enum(int)`
1. `fields.Enum(fields.String)`
2. `fields.Enum(MyEnumClass)`

it wouldn't really have to do much except fetch via attrname.name or attrname.value** instead of just attrname by default, and, if the enum class is available, autogenerate a OneOf validator expr. It should also appear from the outside (e.g. to consumers like apispec) to be of whatever type the actual value is (i.e. the type of the name or the type of the value, depending).

I've never made a custom field before & don't really know how it's done so it would be awesome if somebody more skilled could do this for me, but I have a feeling I'll have to submit my own PR after figuring it all out <_curses under breath_>

*Haskell nerds: I use the term 'kind' advisedly

**Perhaps by-name & by-value would be 2 separate kinds

issue_title: Deserialization of nested fields
How to use Marshmallow when models have __init__?
issue_body:
I'm using Flask and SQL Alchemy for my web app.

This app was already working, but for simplicity I want to use marshmallow for serialize/deserialize.

I set up my schemas and started testing, but I have found that if my model has `__init__` defined without ALL of the model fields defined I can't deserialize due to missing arguments.

Is it a requirement that if `__init__()` is defined that all columns in the db model must be declared?

My model:
```python
class Item(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'items'
    id = db.Column(db.Integer(), primary_key=True)
    project_id = db.Column(db.Integer(), db.ForeignKey('projects.id'))
    myvalue = db.Column(db.Integer())

def __init__(self, project_id=None):
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.myvalue = 0
```

Error:
```python
  File "C:\Users\user\PythonVirtualEnvs\cvirt\lib\site-packages\marshmallow_sqlalchemy\schema.py", line 175, in make_instance
    return self.opts.model(**data)
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'myvalue'
```

It looks like if your model is using `__init__` that you MUST
A. Have all possible db fields defined
B. Include `**kwargs` in the init
C. Do not use `__init__`

 I couldn't find this anywhere in the documentation.

issue_title: Python 3 code being installed by pip into Python 2.7 environment
issue_body:
Installing the current "pyserial" with pip results in an attempt to compile Python 3 code with Python 2. Note 

def open_serial_connection(*,"

which is not legal Python 2.7. Bare "*" for parameters is a Python 3 only feature.

Pyserial documentation claims, at "https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/master/CHANGES.rst", that "The source code is compatible to the 2.x and 3.x series without any changes.", but this is not the case.

While the test below was an upgrade, the same problem occurs on a clean install of pyserial on a new machine.

> sudo pip install pyserial --upgrade
> Downloading/unpacking pyserial from https://pypi.python.org/packages/aa/e7/e3109a5db1d76f459987ee3def5a34cd4ea825ac2320bd0704a8189cbc15/pyserial-3.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl#md5=dd199d56fbb257b3277690bcb8591f36
>   Downloading pyserial-3.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl (93kB): 93kB downloaded
> Installing collected packages: pyserial
>   Found existing installation: pyserial 2.6
>     Not uninstalling pyserial at /usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages, owned by OS
> Compiling /tmp/pip_build_root/pyserial/serial/aio.py ...
>   File "/tmp/pip_build_root/pyserial/serial/aio.py", line 366
>     def open_serial_connection(*,
>                                 ^
> SyntaxError: invalid syntax
> 
> Successfully installed pyserial
> Cleaning up...

issue_title: Using Pyserial as external library for a QGIS plugin
issue_body:
Hello,
I'm developing a python plugin that uses the pySerial library. Installing external libraries in the python folder of QGIS is of course not very complicated, but I'm not sure that this task would be as obvious for every user, especially non-programmers(it's a bit tricky because QGIS keeps its own python folder, and pip doesn't automatically install ext libs there). When an external library is missing, we get an error message as soon as QGIS starts (provoked by the "import" command) and the plugin can't be loaded.
I was wondering if it is possible to include the pyserial code directly in the plugin, and be able to use it without installing it.

Yours,

Nariman Hatami.
INRAP

issue_title: My exported trained model returns wrong predictions once in Core ML format

issue_title: Rewrite Resource.get_chema to not call Resource.get_object_list
issue_body:
Currently if you attempt to access a schema endpoint, `Resource.get_object_list` is called.

If you have a list endpoint, it presumably defines `get_object_list`, thus you incur the cost of that endpoint when retrieving the schema.

If you don't have a list endpoint (i.e. `list_allowed_methods` is empty) this raises `NotImplementedError`! So you have to provide the following `get_object_list` definition for a list-less resource simply so its schema can be accessed:

```
def get_object_list(self, request):
  return []
```

issue_body:

Previously, it would check if there was related_obj, and bypass the rest of the saving code if it didn't. Now, however, it seems to assume there's always a related_obj.

Whenever I attempt to update the resource that field is defined on (specifically, other fields than the GenericForeignkeyField), I receive the error `no resource for model <type 'NoneType'>`.

issue_title: train_ssd.py uses a global variable inconsistently
issue_body:
[train_ssd.py](https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-cv/blob/master/scripts/detection/ssd/train_ssd.py) passes the `ctx` variable as a parameter to the `validate` function, but uses a global `ctx` variable in the `train` function:

```
def train(net, train_data, val_data, eval_metric, args):
    """Training pipeline"""
    net.collect_params().reset_ctx(ctx)
```

The global `ctx` is defined in the `if __name__=="__main__"` block, which is confusing in itself.

This doesn't break the code but actually confused me when I started to use mxnet/gluon/gluoncv. It makes it impossible to use the `train` function from e.g. Jupyter notebooks, whereas `validate` can be used that way.

issue_title: Crash on 64-bit build run on Windows 8.1 when linked with /DYNAMICBASE and /HIGHENTROPYVA
issue_body:
In a 64-bit Windows build of z3 with the link options /DYNAMICBASE and /HIGHENTROPYVA, z3 crashes on the following SMT2 input when run on a Windows 8.1 machine:

(set-info :status unknown)
(declare-fun out@0 () (_ BitVec 3))
(declare-fun reset_N@0 () Bool)
(declare-fun reset_N@1 () Bool)
(assert
(let ((?x16 (ite reset_N@0 (ite (bvule (_ bv4 3) out@0) (_ bv1 3) (bvadd out@0 (_ bv1 3))) (_ bv0 3))))
(let (($x21 (bvule (ite reset_N@1 (ite (bvule (_ bv4 3) ?x16) (_ bv1 3) (bvadd ?x16 (_ bv1 3))) (_ bv0 3)) (_ bv4 3))))
(not $x21))))
(check-sat)

The crash does not occur when the link option /HIGHENTROPYVA:NO is used instead, nor does it occur when the same executable is run on Windows 7.  As far as we can tell, the most likely cause of this is a 64 bit pointer->32 bit->64 bit conversion somewhere in the z3 code that is stripping the high-order bits of the pointer, combined with Windows 8 putting more data than Windows 7 does above the 32-bit barrier when /HIGHENTROPYVA is on.  We are concerned about this for our customers who require a 64-bit Windows version of our program to hold all their data - they may run into random crashes if z3 allocates data above the 32-bit barrier, regardless of the link settings used, unless this issue is addressed.  (In other words, our major concern isn't how to run this exact test case without crashing, but making sure that z3 is truly 64-bit clean.)

We are compiling with Visual C++ 2013.

issue_title: Provide RNN "containers"
issue_body:
In https://github.com/Lasagne/Recipes/pull/23#issuecomment-141979978, @skaae brought up the idea of splitting up the recurrent layers into layers performing a single computation step and a container layer that handles the recurrence (i.e., sets up the `scan` function or explicitly unrolls it).

The use case was to provide a way to efficiently sample from an RNN that was trained to generate time sequences (e.g., text) -- the most efficient way would be to tap into the scan function, but that's impossible the way it's currently encapsulated.
Another advantage could be that it reduces the amount of code duplication (see #356), and maybe it would even make it easier to equip LSTMs or GRUs with custom connections (#357)?

As far as I've understood, the following current code:
```
layer = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(shape=(N_BATCH, MAX_LENGTH, 2))
layer = lasagne.layers.RecurrentLayer(layer, N_HIDDEN)
layer = lasagne.layers.SliceLayer(layer, -1, 1)
layer = lasagne.layers.DenseLayer(layer, num_units=1, nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh)
```
Could be turned into:
```
layer = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(shape=(N_BATCH, MAX_LENGTH, 2))
# single time step
rnn_layer = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((N_BATCH, 2))
rnn_layer = lasagne.layers.VanillaRecurrentStep(rnn_layer, N_HIDDEN)
layer = lasagne.layers.Recurrence(layer, step=rnn_layer)
layer = lasagne.layers.SliceLayer(layer, -1, 1)
layer = lasagne.layers.DenseLayer(layer, num_units=1, nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh)
```
Where we'd have different `Step` layers, and one of them could be sampling from a multivariate distribution, for the character generation use case.
`Recurrence` could have a keyword argument telling whether it persists its recurrent states, and another telling whether it feeds back on its own output (that would be needed for character generation).

Disclaimer: I've just started looking into RNNs and am not sure what's the best way to break down the code yet -- I'm just opening this issue so there is a place to discuss the idea for those who are into RNNs with possible participation by a future me :)

issue_title: PyNWB Docs not building on ReadTheDocx
issue_body:
## 1) Bug

The PyNWB docs are currently not building on ReadTheDocs. The culprit for the failing build seems to be that the PyNWB docs are being built with Sphinx 1.7.1. The other docs for NWB (e.g, the schema docs) seem to use Sphinx 1.6.5 instead and are still building. There is a current RTD GitHub issue for this here:

https://github.com/rtfd/readthedocs.org/issues/3769

I would say, lets just downgrade to Sphinx 1.6.5 for now.

### Steps to Reproduce

See, e.g., https://readthedocs.org/projects/pynwb/builds/6898276/

Here is what ReadTheDocs is trying to run:

```
 python /home/docs/checkouts/readthedocs.org/user_builds/pynwb/envs/latest/bin/sphinx-build -T -b readthedocs -d _build/doctrees-readthedocs -D language=en . _build/html


Running Sphinx v1.7.1
loading translations [en]... done
making output directory...
loading pickled environment... not yet created
loading intersphinx inventory from http://docs.h5py.org/en/latest/objects.inv...
loading intersphinx inventory from https://docs.python.org/3.5/objects.inv...
loading intersphinx inventory from http://matplotlib.org/objects.inv...
intersphinx inventory has moved: http://matplotlib.org/objects.inv -> https://matplotlib.org/objects.inv
loading intersphinx inventory from http://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/objects.inv...
intersphinx inventory has moved: http://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/objects.inv -> https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/objects.inv
loading intersphinx inventory from http://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/objects.inv...
intersphinx inventory has moved: http://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/objects.inv -> https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/objects.inv
/home/docs/checkouts/readthedocs.org/user_builds/pynwb/envs/latest/lib/python3.5/site-packages/h5py/__init__.py:36: FutureWarning: Conversion of the second argument of issubdtype from `float` to `np.floating` is deprecated. In future, it will be treated as `np.float64 == np.dtype(float).type`.
  from ._conv import register_converters as _register_converters
usage: usage: sphinx-build [OPTIONS] -o <OUTPUT_PATH> <MODULE_PATH> [EXCLUDE_PATTERN, ...]
sphinx-build: error: argument -d/--maxdepth: invalid int value: '_build/doctrees-readthedocs'
Command time: 10s Return: 2

```

The part that's a bit strange here is that ```-d/--maxdepth``` are parameters of ```sphinx-apidoc``` not ```sphinx-build```.  ```sphinx-build``` also has a ```-d``` option, but it expects a path, which looks correct in the call.


## Checklist

- [X] Have you ensured the feature or change was not already [reported](https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/issues/) ?
- [X] Have you included a brief and descriptive title?
- [X] Have you included a clear description of the problem you are trying to solve?
- [X] Have you included a minimal code snippet that reproduces the issue you are encountering?

issue_title: CFGFast: crash in _process_irrational_functions
issue_body:
The line https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/fb96563836d42e006dbc03bacac1e859519bc55f/angr/analyses/cfg_base.py#L1217 crashes CFGFast if `function.startpoint` is None. What surprised me is that the function had one node, nevertheless function.startpoint was empty.
Moving the check
```python
if not function.endpoints:
       # Function should have at least one endpoint
       continue
```
above the offending line causes the symptom to go away because `function.endpoints` is empty.

issue_title: Automatic hooking in unstripped static binaries
issue_body:
It would be handy if angr would hook static binaries when symbols are present. (So we don't have to manually look up each address.)

```python
p.hook(0x4018B0, simuvex.SimProcedures['libc.so.6']['__libc_start_main'])
p.hook(0x422690, simuvex.SimProcedures['libc.so.6']['memcpy'])
p.hook(0x408F10, simuvex.SimProcedures['libc.so.6']['puts'])
```
([Taken from whitehat_crypto400.](https://github.com/angr/angr-doc/blob/2eda67f29a9aed9cb538be41a7cfb12a97beec5c/examples/whitehat_crypto400/solve.py#L25-27))

```
[01:20:55] <dave0x6d> zardus: is there a way to get simmy to automatically hook all the libc stuff in a static binary?
[01:21:38] <zardus> there is not, unfortunately
[01:22:02] <zardus> currently, you'd need to use flirt in IDA and export those symbols
[01:23:03] <dave0x6d> the binary isn't stripped.
[01:23:09] <dave0x6d> I already imported the symbols with bindiff.
[01:23:35] <zardus> oh
[01:23:50] <zardus> there's nothing automatic, though that would be really cool
[01:25:06] <zardus> if you're interested in taking a stab at it, the code would go here: https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/project.py#L164
[01:25:34] <zardus> otherwise, you can open an issue so it's not forgotten :-)
```

I have a binary that's a good test case for this, can't share it at the moment though because the CTF is happening right now. I'll upload it on Monday (just submitting this ticket now so I don't forget.)

issue_title: Support setting yaw rate and yaw acceleration
issue_body:
This feature is needed to preserve a smooth feel in response to target angle change while recording video.  Without it, the scene has a "jerky" feel and the subject move across the field of view instead of staying centered.  Importantly in contrast to upstream discussion that appear to talk about a global setting, this would need to be settable per command.

Some documentation from the various examples in particular; currently this appears to be unsupported but the example documentation might be old and the issues are closed.

### Ardu [documentation](http://ardupilot.org/copter/docs/common-mavlink-mission-command-messages-mav_cmd.html#mav-cmd-condition-yaw) of MAV_CMD_CONDITION_YAW
`The parameters allow you to specify whether the target direction is absolute or relative to the current yaw direction. If the direction is relative you can also (separately) specify whether the value is added or subtracted from the current heading (note that the vehicle will always turn in direction that most quickly gets it to the new target heading regardless of the param3 value).`

`We don’t support controlling the yaw rate, so the param2 value (Sec) is ignored.`

## Dronkekit [examples](http://python.dronekit.io/examples/guided-set-speed-yaw-demo.html)   

### send_global_velocity

        velocity_z, # Z velocity in NED frame in m/s
        0, 0, 0, # afx, afy, afz acceleration (not supported yet, ignored in GCS_Mavlink)
        0, 0)    # yaw, yaw_rate (not supported yet, ignored in GCS_Mavlink)

### condition_yaw

        heading,    # param 1, yaw in degrees
        0,          # param 2, yaw speed deg/s
        1,          # param 3, direction -1 ccw, 1 cw

## Ardu issues 
This is apparently a requested feature upstream. includes ArduPilot/ardupilot#1635, ArduPilot/ardupilot#1758

issue_title: Lots of (un-needed warnings) for new users on first launch.
issue_body:
In summary there are a few things we can do to improve the first launch experience for new users:

 - [ ] There is an IPython problem which will probably concern users of Python 2.6 (on first load, and maybe after this which should be looked at, I suspect we're using an old deprecated API call in ```bootstrap.py```).

 - [ ] Can we replace the GLITE stuff with a 1 line error saying that GLITE functions are not enabled. Users running (on purpose without this) shouldn't be constantly be being told about functionality they don't need/want.

- [ ] If we are suggesting people run from cvmfs should we provide a Python 2.7 binary to them rather than requiring that they have root access in order to install Python 2.7 by hand?

I think these are not too unreasonable to be looked into prior to 6.1.19,

Regards,

Rob

issue_title: Add creation of buckets in permissions endpoint
issue_body:
Currently with these settings:

```ini
kinto.experimental_permissions_endpoint = true
kinto.bucket_create_principals = system.Everyone  # or whatever 
```

I should see the permission to create buckets in http://localhost:8888/v1/permissions

Something like:
```js
{
  "data": [
    {
      "uri": "/buckets",
      "resource_name": "bucket",
      "permissions": [
        "create",
      ],
    }
  ]
}
```

This is because creating bucket is a specific case: it does not involve parents (buckets are top level) and does not involve the permission backend (permission can only be set in `.ini` settings).
Currently we only iterate on existing buckets https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/bf60689ae016a31b8c5bb1955397be46d7b4acca/kinto/views/permissions.py#L91

Would allow to fix https://github.com/Kinto/kinto-admin/issues/401

issue_title: Allow complex dtype of input
issue_body: Presently, the function 'cwt' does not operate on complex input data.  It calls '_check_dtype,' which causes a downcast to float, discarding the imaginary part of the input data. issue_title: Dialect cannot work with huge data tables (allocation of memory on server/client)
Depth image "shrinks" after starting SR300 camera
issue_body:
**Note:** Please take advantage of RealSense [Community](https://communities.intel.com/community/tech/realsense) and [Support](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/support/emerging-technologies/intel-realsense-technology.html) sites.

| Required Info  |   |
|---|---|
| Camera Model | SR300 | 
| Firmware Version | 3.15.0.0  | 
| Operating System & Version | Ubuntu 14.04  |
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)  | 4.4.0-75-generic  |

Hi,

I am having an issue with the SR300 sensor. Just after starting the sensor (e.g. using cpp-capture or cpp-config-ui), then the depth image is almost complete, see first screenshot below.

![cpp_config1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10979365/28644284-f54f4a08-7226-11e7-8c84-7b59a3fc7f87.png)

Then after a few seconds the depth image "shrinks" (there are fewer depth points on the table). This image is taken while the depth image is shrinking.
![cpp_config2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10979365/28644302-fc1c5a06-7226-11e7-84f0-8f89712ab330.png)

After shrinking the depth image looks like this:
![cpp_config3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10979365/28644307-ffd5aa9e-7226-11e7-86a2-2c4659b95f2c.png)

It seems like this is related to the intensity of the IR laser, since the IR image also becomes darker at the same time. I noticed that after adjusting the "LASER POWER" slider to a different value (initially set to 16 and decrease to 15), then the depth image seems "complete" (except some missing pixels at the edge of the object and on the right edge of the depth image).

I also noticed that the "complete" image (first screenshot) has some weird depth reading at the bottom of the box, but the last screenshot shows good depth reading at the bottom of the box (but one side of the box doesn't have depth readings at all). It would be nice to get both a complete depth image (as in first screenshot) and good depth readings on the objects (as in the last screenshot).

Do you have any advice for how to configure the camera, such that the depth image is as complete as possible and the depth image readings are as accurate as possible? I guess there is a trade-off to be made. I am using the sensor for a particular application, where the objects are in range 15-40 cm. I have objects on a table with a camera above them with an angle of about 20 degrees relative to the table.

Thank you.
Uli

issue_title: rs2_create_playback_device is missing
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       |  D400  | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.09.09.02 | 
| Operating System & Version |    MacOS  | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  NA                                        | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            |   master(f0caecdcb2047b968202b18bc9703bce0a74517a)                        | 

### Issue Description
A linking error occurs when using `rs2_create_playback_device`:
```C
rs2_error* e = 0;
rs2_create_playback_device("a.bag", &e);
```
```
Undefined symbols for architecture x86_64:
  "_rs2_create_playback_device", referenced from:
      _main in rs-depth.o
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture x86_64
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
```
I find the [doc](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/src/media#rs2playback) uses `ctx.load_device("my_file_name.bag")` which calls `rs2_context_add_device`, so does this mean `rs2_create_playback_device` has already been deprecated? But `rs2_create_playback_device` is still in the C API [here](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/f0caecdcb2047b968202b18bc9703bce0a74517a/include/librealsense2/h/rs_record_playback.h#L69). Is there an MWE on how to use `rs2_context_add_device`?

Another question is in the [record-playback sample](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/examples/record-playback), I noticed new pipelines are created, configured and deleted in the process of recording, is this the best practice? Instead of stopping, recreating a new pipeline:
https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/f0caecdcb2047b968202b18bc9703bce0a74517a/examples/record-playback/rs-record-playback.cpp#L89-L98

maybe it's sufficient to just create another record device via `rs2_create_record_device`? [My current implementation](https://github.com/Gnimuc/RealSense.jl/blob/882fa3901e017353b02bffddd8722126d916d147/examples/record-playback/rs-record.jl#L121-L150) looks like this:
```
# start recording
record_dev = rs2_create_record_device(dev, "a.bag", &e)
# pause recording
rs2_record_device_pause(record_dev, &e)
# resume recording
rs2_record_device_resume(record_dev, &e)
# stop recording
rs2_delete_device(record_dev)
```
and it also works. 

I'm very confused by these record-playback related APIs, I'm wondering whether should we use `pipeline` APIs or `device` APIs in this specific case. The [API Architecture doc](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/master/doc/api_arch.md) mentions two level of APIs, but it's not crystal clear to me that which API is high-level, which is not.

issue_title: atwf and "files" serialization of workflows doesn't make it easy to set vasp command
issue_body:
@bocklund @shyuep - this relates to your part of atomate

## Summary

* The documentation for atomate suggests that users create their first workflow using the command: ```atwf add -l vasp -s optimize_only.yaml -m mp-149```
* Unfortunately, this command does not honor the "vasp_cmd" set up in my_fworker.yaml and just runs "vasp" as the executable (making the set up of my_fworker.yaml pointless)
* I don't see any easy way for the user to put in their vasp_cmd. They can edit optimize_only.yaml to add a ``common_params`` but that is clunky - they need to locate the file and edit it. I actually think it less of a pain to just use Python in that case.
* This has led to user failures in trying to follow the atomate tutorials

## Example code

See this thread:
https://groups.google.com/forum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer#!topic/atomate/xVghmE3YOco

## Suggested solution (if any)

Possible solutions include:

* Replace the docs command with the one suggested in the Google Groups thread (```atwf add -l vasp -p wf_bandstructure -m mp-149```). This will at least stop initial users from seeing errors right away but then the tutorial is more about preset function based workflows than file-based workflows
* Edit all the file-based workflows in the library so that the common_params has vasp_cmd set to ">>vasp_cmd<<". Kind of a pain and everyone that needs to do this for all file-based workflows
* Allow atwf to set common_params during the command line command itself. Something similar to (``atwf add -l vasp -s optimize_only.yaml -m mp-149 --common_params vasp_cmd=">>vasp_cmd<<"). Again a little clunky and non-intuitive, but it would work

There are probably other solutions but those are 3 possibilities

issue_body:
Test that repos can be created and synced with mirrorlist --feed
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2224
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/175

1. pulp-admin -vv rpm repo create --repo-id mirror --feed "http://mirrorlist.centos.org/?release=7&arch=x86_64&repo=os"  --relative-url mirror

2. pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id=mirror

Assert that

1. Repo create is successful with the --relative-url 
2. Repo create is successful without --relative-url
3. Repo can be synced with relative-url
4. Repo can be synced without relative-url
5. Repo can be published with relative-url
7. Repo can be synced with lazy settings (on_demand,background)

issue_title: subproc_vec_env stacking shouldn't always use numpy arrays
issue_body:
`subproc_vec_env` currently uses `np.stack` to stack observations into a tensor. This works for atari, but doesn't generalize well to environments where observations are eg: dicts. I want to build an environment where the agent gets both an image and a text string as an observation. It's fairly impractical/annoying to be constrained to merging these together into a big tensor.

I would like to propose that for a dict or tuple observation space, it would make more sense to just put the observations together in a Python array instead. This could be done automatically by `subproc_vec_env`, or by a new, more general vec env.

I really think more work, such as this, should be put into making the OpenAI baselines generalize to environments outside of eg: atari.

issue_title: The description for GraphQLInt is incorrect
issue_body:
The current description on `GraphQLInt`, as depicted [here](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/master/graphql/type/scalars.py#L39-L42), says:

> The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric 
> values. Int can represent values between -(2^53 - 1) and 2^53 - 1 since
> represented in JSON as double-precision floating point numbers specified
> by [IEEE 754](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

However, the [current implementation](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/master/graphql/type/scalars.py#L23-L27) complies to [GraphQL spec](http://facebook.github.io/graphql/October2016/#sec-Int), which confines `GraphQLInt` to between `-2^-31` and `2^31-1`.

If we all agree that the description is incorrect and should be updated, I can submit a pull request to update the descriptions to reflect that.

issue_title: Memoize Validation results
issue_body: We see significant (~50%) of our page latency being taken up by query validation, it'd be nice to memoize this validation step to avoid recalculating it. This is mentioning in the GraphQL spec http://facebook.github.io/graphql/#sec-Validation, and would need some thought to ensure it is purged correctly on schema updates, but could lead to significant speed improvements in our use case.

issue_title: Empty IP logged when using IPv4 behind reverse proxy
issue_body:
When running behind a reverse proxy, [this line](https://github.com/jazzband/django-axes/blob/ef3d527bee81e10455f73e57aaea13a222c9b792/axes/decorators.py#L43) ```python ip = request.META.get(REVERSE_PROXY_HEADER, '').split(',', 1)[0] ``` correctly gets my external IP, e.g. `208.67.222.222`. Since it's not IPv6, [this code](https://github.com/jazzband/django-axes/blob/ef3d527bee81e10455f73e57aaea13a222c9b792/axes/decorators.py#L57) runs: ```python if not is_ipv6(ip): # Fix for IIS adding client port number to 'HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR' header (removes port number). ip = ''.join(ip.split(':')[:-1]) ``` and sets `ip = ''`, which is then returned, resulting in this logged access attempt:
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/199071/20320696/a3114f8c-ab27-11e6-8e37-a0f09f267c42.png) I'm not using IIS.

issue_title: Unexpected categorizer output regarding underscores
issue_body:
Migrated from https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/9053.

The following script reproduces:
```py
import tensorflow as tf

def main():
  k = tf.constant(0)
  tf.summary.scalar('loss', k)
  tf.summary.scalar('loss/regularization', k)
  tf.summary.scalar('loss_extra_stuff', k)
  summ = tf.summary.merge_all()

  sess = tf.Session()
  writer = tf.summary.FileWriter('/tmp/cat2')
  writer.add_graph(sess.graph)
  writer.add_summary(sess.run(summ))
  writer.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
  main()
```

In TensorBoard, the categorizer emits two categories called "loss", one with tag "loss" and one with "loss/regularization". This should not happen. It does not occur if the third summary is removed.

issue_body:
5.3.7 fails due to IPv6 source address is not the Rloc of DUT as a leader.(::ff:fe00:0)
Test Script will check if the source address of Address Error Coap packet is fd00::ff:fe00:0 as below:
"stepResults.update(ScriptHelper.validate_CoAP_Address_Error_Packet(mleAdvtPacket, strKey="3",checkAllTLVs=True,Ipv6_Src_Address_Check ="::ff:fe00:0"))"

Currently we use random router id when allocating, even for leader router id. Hence, above criteria fails.

Enhancement:
